application_rule: Extract a method rule, decision procedure, bias-control rule, or workflow constraint; then make the component obey that rule.
output_it_must_change: method rule, stage design, instrument behavior, or analytic procedure
test_or_check: A reviewer can identify the source-derived rule and see where the component enforces it.

application_rule: Extract actor logic, historical constraint, domain vocabulary, empirical pattern, or case condition; then prevent generic page language.
output_it_must_change: domain variables, case framing, project examples, terminology, or scenario fixtures
test_or_check: The project would be visibly wrong or thinner if the domain source were removed.

application_rule: Extract the data flow, system behavior, software constraint, retrieval path, scoring logic, or failure mode that must become visible.
output_it_must_change: module contract, data contract, proof artifact, runtime trace, or failure-handling rule
test_or_check: The component shows how the mechanism works, not only what it is called.

application_rule: Extract attacker incentives, deception timing, poisoning pathway, saturation tactic, evasion strategy, or failure pressure.
output_it_must_change: attack fixture, red-team lane, defensive gate, warning condition, or replay scenario
test_or_check: The proof can show what the adversary does and where the system detects, absorbs, narrows, or blocks it.

application_rule: Extract observable criteria: pass/fail condition, reliability check, coding rule, calibration test, or release gate.
output_it_must_change: test suite, acceptance criteria, scorecard, confidence rule, or release decision
test_or_check: A reviewer can determine whether the component passed or failed without relying on impressionistic language.

application_rule: Extract dissent, uncertainty, scope condition, failure mode, or rival interpretation; then narrow public claims.
output_it_must_change: claim ceiling, caveat, blocked claim, rival interpretation, or uncertainty statement
test_or_check: The public claim becomes more disciplined because this source was applied.

application_rule: State exactly which page, chapter, module, output, decision, or sentence the source changes.
output_it_must_change: visible public copy, proof engine behavior, chapter decision, module field, or exported artifact
test_or_check: A before/after or trace record shows source material changing a concrete output.

application_rule: Identify which passages, pages, or concepts must be read before quotation, citation, or stronger literature-positioning is allowed.
output_it_must_change: close-read note, citation record, passage ledger, or blocked-claim release condition
test_or_check: No strong citation claim is released until passage-level evidence exists.
